model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: This projects as one of the tighter Round of 32 games in the bracket, but Arkansas gets a slight edge because its predictive profile is marginally stronger overall, with top-five offensive efficiency in both KenPom and Torvik and a small overall rating advantage over Wisconsin. Wisconsin absolutely has a credible path if it controls tempo and turns this into a disciplined half-court game, especially since the teams are very close statistically. Still, with Arkansas carrying the slightly higher ceiling and no clearly confirmed availability issue strong enough to swing the matchup, the Razorbacks are the narrow pick.

reasoningStep.title: Compared near-equal efficiency profiles and checked for health flags
reasoningStep.summary: Arkansas and Wisconsin are extremely close in the predictive metrics, but Arkansas holds the slightly better overall ranking and the better offensive ceiling. Wisconsin’s recent injury-related news creates at least a little more uncertainty on its side, even though no decisive absence was confirmed.
evidence: KenPom: Arkansas No. 17 (AdjEM 25.43, AdjO 127.6 No. 5, AdjD 102.1) vs Wisconsin No. 22 (AdjEM 23.4, AdjO 125.4 No. 11, AdjD 102.0).
Torvik: Arkansas No. 20 (AdjO 127.77 No. 5, AdjD 101.98 No. 50) vs Wisconsin No. 21 (AdjO 127.20 No. 8, AdjD 101.62 No. 48).

Web search surfaced Wisconsin injury-update reporting around Nolan Winter and Jack Janicki ahead of the tournament, while no comparably specific major Arkansas absence was clearly confirmed in the returned results.
Wisconsin has a credible upset path because the teams are nearly identical defensively and close overall, which keeps confidence low.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Round: Round of 32
